macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: U.S. Strikes Iran After Drone Attack on Cargo Ship in Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: CENTCOM confirmed U.S. aircraft hit Iran's missile and drone storage locations Friday following an Iranian drone attack on a cargo ship Thursday, escalating regional tensions and impacting oil markets.

items.title: Nvidia's Market Cap Falls Below $5 Trillion Amid AI Chip Sell-Off
items.summary: Nvidia's market capital dropped below $5 trillion as major tech indexes declined, with S&P futures down 0.25%, Nasdaq off 0.6%, and Dow futures falling 0.53% ahead of key jobs data.

items.title: Honda Halts Plans for Electric Vehicle Factory in Canada
items.summary: Honda announced it will put a hold on its plans to establish an electric vehicle factory in Canada, citing shifting global trade tensions and U.S. tariff announcements affecting North American manufacturing.

items.title: U.S. Non-Farm Payrolls Surge to 312K, Beating Expectations
items.summary: The latest U.S. jobs report showed Non-Farm Payrolls at +312K versus the expected +185K, while the unemployment rate held at 3.6%, driving mixed reactions in futures markets with S&P down 0.4%.

items.title: Former Trump Adviser John Bolton Pleads Guilty to Mishandling Classified Documents
items.summary: John Bolton pleaded guilty to mishandling classified documents, facing up to five years in prison and agreeing to a $2.25 million fine, adding to recent political and legal developments.

items.title: Eurozone Manufacturing PMI Hits 36-Month High at 49.80 Points
items.summary: The Eurozone manufacturing PMI reached 49.80 points in July, a 36-month high, while annual inflation remained unchanged at 2%, signaling potential stabilization in European economic activity.

items.title: New U.S. Tariffs Trigger Risk-Off Sentiment in European Markets
items.summary: European markets closed lower as new Trump tariffs triggered a wave of risk-off sentiment, with Asia stocks also falling amid weak China data and the implementation of new tariff rates.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: $590 Million in Crypto Futures Liquidations Hit Market in Past 24 Hours
items.summary: The cryptocurrency derivatives market saw $590 million in total futures liquidations, with BTC leading at $28.6 million, followed by SOL ($21.12M), ETH ($17.2M), and XRP ($7.35M), according to MyToken data.

items.title: Bitcoin Falls to 20-Month Low Near $40,010 Amid AI Capital Rotation
items.summary: Bitcoin dropped 7.5% over 24 hours to approximately $40,010, marking its lowest level in 20 months, as speculative capital steadily rotates toward AI projects; US Bitcoin ETFs saw $6.4 billion in outflows in May.

items.title: Binance Delists Four Altcoins: ALCX, ARDR, NFP, and POND
items.summary: Binance announced the termination of services for Alchemix (ALCX), Ardor (ARDR), NFPrompt Token (NFP), and Marlin (POND), with delisting scheduled for July 10; NFP plunged 21% immediately following the announcement.

items.title: Binance Faces MiCA Regulatory Pressure in EU, May Seek License Outside Greece
items.summary: Binance is reportedly considering applying for its EU MiCA license in a country other than Greece amid regulatory challenges; users in Poland, Italy, Spain, and France have been advised to withdraw funds.

items.title: Record $10.6 Billion Bitcoin Options Expiry Triggers Volatility
items.summary: The largest Bitcoin options expiry in history, totaling roughly $10.6 billion, occurred today, contributing to market volatility; analysts note a rare on-chain signal alignment suggesting Bitcoin may be near a major inflection point.

items.title: Bitcoin's 53% Drawdown Is Shallowest Bear Market on Record
items.summary: Cointelegraph reports that Bitcoin's current 53% drawdown is the shallowest bear market in its history, significantly smaller than previous declines of 77% to 93%.

items.title: BNY Integrates Chainalysis for Digital Asset Compliance
items.summary: BNY, the world's largest custodian bank, has selected Chainalysis as the compliance foundation for its digital asset platform, marking the first Global Systemically Important Bank to do so.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Qualcomm Acquires AI Software Firm Modular in $3.9B Deal to Accelerate AI
items.summary: Qualcomm agreed to acquire Modular, an AI software company, for approximately $3.9 billion in a stock deal aimed at making artificial intelligence faster and cheaper for its customers.

items.title: OpenAI Limits Access to New Models Amid Government Security Review
items.summary: OpenAI has restricted access to its latest AI models citing White House security concerns, while the Trump administration recently rolled back part of a ban on Anthropic's Mythos 5 model for trusted partners.

items.title: OpenAI and Broadcom Develop Custom 'Jalapeño' Chip for AI Inference
items.summary: OpenAI and Broadcom have built a new custom chip named Jalapeño designed to improve efficiency and lower costs for AI inference, advancing OpenAI's full-stack strategy.

items.title: New 'Open Sinker Agent 32B' Model Outperforms Benchmarks in Coding and Medical Tasks
items.summary: A new research project, OpenThoughts-Agent, released the Open Sinker Agent 32B model (based on Qwen 3 32B), which reportedly outperforms other models across financial, terminal, medical, and software engineering benchmarks.

items.title: Anthropic Accuses Alibaba of Illicitly Accessing Frontier Model Claude
items.summary: U.S. AI lab Anthropic has accused Chinese e-commerce giant Alibaba of illicitly accessing its frontier model Claude to harvest its capabilities, raising new security concerns.

items.title: SoftBank's Masayoshi Son Sets $6.189 Trillion Net Asset Target on 'Super AI' Promise
items.summary: SoftBank CEO Masayoshi Son announced a bold goal to raise SoftBank's net asset value to $6.189 trillion over the next decade, driven by his promise of 'Super AI'.

items.title: New Coalition Launches Sweeping Strategy to Prepare Millions for AI Career Upheaval
items.summary: A coalition of employers and state governments is developing a comprehensive strategy to help workers adapt to the AI age and respond to potential career upheavals.

items.title: Study Finds Large Language Models May Not Outperform Market Over Long Periods
items.summary: A new study casts doubt on the long-term efficacy of large language models, finding they may work well initially but do not outperform the market over long periods or in changing conditions.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Steam Summer Sale Kicks Off with GTA 6 Preorders and New Game Releases
items.summary: The Steam Summer Sale has begun, featuring preorders for Grand Theft Auto 6 at $80 (regular) and $100 (Ultimate Edition), alongside new releases like High On Life 2 (Switch 2 on July 1), Sand Raiders of Sophie (early access), and Impulse (live-service shooter).

items.title: Sony Confirms Bungie Layoffs and GTA 5 Free Upgrade for PS4/Xbox One Owners
items.summary: Sony has announced layoffs at Bungie, while Rockstar Games revealed that the current-gen upgrade pass for GTA 5 will be free for PS4 and Xbox One owners. Additionally, the narrative shooter Lunar Abyss saw its entire team laid off less than a month post-release.
items.source: YouTube (Sony brings down the axe on Bungie + GTA VI pricing)

items.title: Nintendo Switch 2 Dominates Hardware Sales; GTA 6 PS5 Preorders Outsell Xbox 6-to-1
items.summary: Nintendo Switch 2 reached 5.9 million U.S. installed base in 12 months, leading hardware sales. Internal IGN data shows GTA 6 PS5 preorders are outselling Xbox by a 6-to-1 ratio, while High On Life 2 launches on Switch 2 July 1 with a new demo available.

items.title: Xbox Consoles Price Hike in August; Lords of the Fallen 2 Delayed to Q1 2027
items.summary: Xbox Series X prices will increase by $100 (512 GB) and $150 (1 TB) starting August 1, with a digital Series X rising from $600 to $750. Lords of the Fallen 2 has been delayed from Q3 2026 to Q1 2027, ahead of GTA 6's release.

items.title: Circana May 2026 Report: U.S. Game Spending Up 4%, Switch 2 Leads, PS5 Spending Falls 43%
items.summary: U.S. video game spending reached $23.0 billion in 2026 (4% higher than 2025). Nintendo Switch 2 was the best-selling hardware, while PS5 spending fell 43% and unit sales dropped 58%. 007 First Light was May's top-selling game, with Resident Evil: Requiem leading 2026 YTD.

items.title: Nintendo Raises Employee Salaries by 10%; PAYDAY 2 Gets Engine Upgrade
items.summary: Nintendo announced a 10% salary increase for employees, while PAYDAY 2 is receiving an improved engine and 64-bit architecture update, enhancing performance and stability for the popular co-op shooter.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: No AI-Excluded Tech News Found in Past 24 Hours
items.summary: The available search results from June 26–27, 2026, contain no major product launches, company news, or tech industry developments that exclude AI-specific content; all relevant tech mentions focus on AI safety, generative AI, autonomous AI systems, or AI factories.
